evals.prompt: We have a Next.js 14 App Router project at /tmp/test-nextjs. It has no localization setup. Integrate it with the Blocks Localization Microservice. Use the blocks-uilm skill. The API base URL is https://api.blocks.example.com and project key is TEST_KEY_123.
evals.expected_output: Complete i18next + React Query setup: language.types.ts, language.service.ts with getUilmFile/getLanguages/getModules, i18n.ts with key-mode toggle, LanguageProvider.tsx with dual-module loading, LanguageSelector.tsx, route-module-map.ts, and root layout updated.
expectations: The skill runs the pre-flight audit and detects Next.js App Router
A LanguageProvider is created wrapping children in QueryClientProvider
language.service.ts uses x-blocks-key header for auth
getUilmFile returns flat Record<string, string> directly (not wrapped in data.translations)
Dual-module loading loads common + route-specific modules via route-module-map.ts
i18n.ts has window.__i18nKeyMode flag and postMessage listener for browser extension
useTranslation() is called with no namespace argument

evals.prompt: Set up languages and translation keys for a project. Add English (en) and German (de) as languages with English as default. Create common and auth modules. Then add LOGIN_TITLE key with English and German translations in the auth module.
evals.expected_output: Languages and modules created via save-language and save-module actions. Translation key created via save-key action.
expectations: Calls save-language action for English and German with correct field names (languageName, languageCode, projectKey) and isDefault/isRTL
Calls set-default-language action with languageName field (not languageId or itemId)
Calls save-module action for common and auth modules with correct field name (moduleName) for module operations
Calls save-key action for LOGIN_TITLE with moduleId from the auth module and resources[] array containing {value, culture, characterLength} objects
Uses moduleId for key operations and moduleName for module operations — these are different identifiers
Key names follow semantic naming: AUTH_LOGIN_TITLE, ERROR_REQUIRED — not BTN_LOGIN, HERO_TITLE

evals.prompt: Import a JSON translation file for French into the dashboard module. The file is at /tmp/fr-dashboard.json and contains { HERO_TITLE: 'Bienvenue', NAV_HOME: 'Accueil' }. The file must first be uploaded to UILM to obtain a fileId, then import-uilm is called with that fileId.
evals.expected_output: File uploaded (fileId obtained), then import-uilm called with fileId reference. Import completed via import-uilm action.
expectations: Explains that import-uilm uses fileId — NOT raw JSON body and NOT multipart file upload
Describes the upload-then-import two-step flow: upload file to get fileId, then call import-uilm with fileId
If demonstrating the request body: includes { projectKey, fileId, messageCoRelationId } — not the raw JSON content
File format uploaded must be flat JSON: { KEY: value }
Handles 400 error if module, language, or fileId doesn't exist

evals.prompt: Scan the src/components directory for translation keys. Find all useTranslation() calls and extract the keys passed to t(). Generate a CSV file with all found keys in the common module format.
evals.expected_output: Single CSV file generated with columns: ItemId, ModuleId, Module, KeyName, en-US, de-DE, fr-FR. All keys across all modules in one file. Keys extracted from t() calls. Keys in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. Every string value must be double-quoted.
expectations: Scans .tsx and .ts files in the target directory
Extracts keys from t('KEY_NAME') calls
Skips dynamic keys like t(variableName)
Deduplicates extracted keys
Generates single CSV with correct format: ItemId empty, ModuleId empty, Module populated per key
Each data row has EXACTLY 2 commas before the module name — not 0, 1, 3, or 4
Every string value in the CSV is wrapped in double quotes, even short words like 'Features' or 'Sign In'
Module column (3rd data column) is never empty — common/home/auth/dashboard etc.
Source language column filled with extracted values, target columns empty
Renames keys to semantic names: NAV_HOME, HOME_HERO_TITLE — not BTN_HOME, FOOTER_PRODUCT

evals.prompt: Debug why translations are showing key names instead of translated values in the app. The user reports that t('HERO_TITLE') displays 'HERO_TITLE' instead of the actual text. Check if key-mode debugging is enabled.
evals.expected_output: Key-mode debugging issue identified. window.__i18nKeyMode flag checked and fixed. Translations restored.
expectations: Checks window.__i18nKeyMode flag value
Identifies that flag is true when it should be false
Sets window.__i18nKeyMode = false
Triggers re-render via i18n.emit('languageChanged', language)
Explains that the UILM browser extension sent a keymode:true message

evals.prompt: The app is loading all translations on every route change, causing slow page loads. Optimize by implementing dual-module loading so only common + route-specific modules load per page.
evals.expected_output: Dual-module loading implemented. route-module-map.ts created. LanguageProvider updated to load only needed modules.
expectations: Creates route-module-map.ts with getRouteModuleName() and getModulesForRoute()
Always loads 'common' module on every route
Loads route-specific module derived from pathname (e.g., 'home' for /)
Falls back to ['common', 'home'] for unknown routes
getUilmFile called per module, not a single bulk fetch
No duplicate modules loaded for the same route

evals.prompt: An existing Angular project already uses ngx-translate. Migrate it to the Blocks UILM microservice without breaking the existing translation system. The project uses @ngx-translate/core with HTTP loader.
evals.expected_output: BlocksTranslateLoader created replacing the HTTP loader. Translations fetched from UILM getUilmFile per module. Local files kept as fallback.
expectations: Creates BlocksTranslateLoader using UILM getUilmFile API
Uses x-blocks-key header for auth
getUilmFile returns flat Record<string, string> directly
Keeps local files as offline fallback
Imports BlocksTranslateLoader in app.config.ts

evals.prompt: The translation key AUTH_LOGIN_TITLE exists in the auth module but has no German translation. Use AI to auto-translate it to German. The key's itemId is key-123. Use the translate-key action.
evals.expected_output: translate-key action called with keyId (itemId), messageCoRelationId (UUID), defaultLanguage, and projectKey. Translation completed via AI.
expectations: Calls translate-key action (not translate-all — this is a single key)
Uses keyId field with the itemId value from get-keys
Generates and includes a valid UUID for messageCoRelationId (required field)
Uses defaultLanguage: 'en' (the source language)
Uses projectKey from environment/config
All four fields present in the request body

evals.prompt: The common module has 50 keys in English but most languages haven't been translated yet. Use AI to translate all untranslated keys in the common module at once. The common module's moduleId is module-456.
evals.expected_output: translate-all action called with moduleId, messageCoRelationId, defaultLanguage, and projectKey. All untranslated keys translated.
expectations: Calls translate-all action (not translate-key — this is bulk translation)
Uses moduleId field (not moduleName) with the moduleId from get-modules
Generates and includes a valid UUID for messageCoRelationId (required field)
Uses defaultLanguage: 'en' (the source language)
Uses projectKey from environment/config
All four fields present in the request body

evals.prompt: Set up a webhook so the app receives notifications when translations change. The webhook URL is https://myapp.example.com/webhooks/localization. Use HMAC signature verification with secret 'my-secret-xyz' and header 'x-webhook-sig'.
evals.expected_output: Webhook configured via save-webhook action. Blocking webhook returns success.
expectations: Calls save-webhook action
Request body includes url, contentType: 'application/json', isDisabled: false
blocksWebhookSecret is a nested object: { secret: 'my-secret-xyz', headerKey: 'x-webhook-sig' }
Uses projectKey from environment/config
Omits itemId when creating (include it only when updating)
